channel.title: The Grant Desk - Cross-disciplinary & Social Impact, next 90 days
channel.description: Paid open calls, fellowships and residencies in AI, tech, research, and digital and mixed-media arts, sorted into the right pile. Updated as new calls land on the desk. Filtered to: Cross-disciplinary & Social Impact, next 90 days.

item.title: Global Artivism: Governance Fellow
item.description:
Organisation: Global Artivism (a fiscally sponsored project of Res Publica US Inc.)

Location: Remote, open globally. Priority given to applicants from the Global South.

Award: USD 1,200 to 1,800 per month (independent contractor fee), paid on receipt of monthly invoices. No employee benefits, paid leave, or insurance; contractor covers their own taxes and compliance.

Duration: 12-month independent contractor engagement from August 2026, with potential renewal subject to funding and mutual agreement. Flexible hours with scheduled calls across time zones.

Deadline: 16 Jul 2026

Global Artivism, a Global Majority-led movement that engages the power of arts and culture toward a just world on a safe planet, is contracting a Governance Fellow for a mentored, time-limited engagement focused on governance systems, democratic institutional design, and Global Majority-led movement infrastructure. The contractor supports the development and maintenance of Global Artivism's governance architecture, including documentation, committee coordination, and research that supports the Democratic Transition process, and is expected to take initiative on assigned tasks and grow through structured mentorship. The movement is governed by a Global Council with a Movement Secretariat coordination team; the Fellow coordinates primarily with the Governance Lead alongside the Operations Director, Secretariat members, and Council committees (coordination relationships do not constitute supervisory authority). KEY RESPONSIBILITIES: governance documentation (frameworks, committee charters, terms of reference, process documentation; maintaining an organized, current, consistently formatted document library; preparing materials for Global Council meetings); committee coordination (scheduling, materials, meeting notes, follow-up, trackers of decisions and action items, agendas and briefing packs); Democratic Transition and electoral process support (research on governance models, electoral processes, and democratic participation mechanisms; drafting materials for the Democratic Transition Roadmap 2025-2027; preparing electoral framework and testing documentation; tracking milestones); and governance research (comparative analyses and briefing notes; staying current on distributed governance, participatory democracy, and decentralized leadership; contributing to knowledge products). REQUIRED: manages multiple documentation tasks and meets deadlines with attention to accuracy and formatting (experience via union organizing, participatory facilitation, cooperative governance, or community-based decision-making counts alongside academic or formal work history); good written English; ability to take initiative and work independently; genuine interest in governance, movement building, or institutional design; ability to operate as an independent contractor; and commitment to Global Artivism's values. PREFERRED: background in governance, law, political science, or international development; interest in Global Majority leadership and distributed power; civil society/NGO/movement experience; familiarity with arts, culture, or social justice movements; multilingual ability in languages relevant to Global South contexts. This is an independent contractor engagement managed through Global Artivism's fiscal sponsorship with Res Publica US Inc.; the contractor sets their own hours and methods, may serve other clients, and handles their own taxes and local compliance. TO APPLY: submit an expression of interest via the ABM Intelligence application page; submissions are reviewed on a rolling basis by ABM's consulting team. Expressions of interest close Thursday 16 July 2026, 10pm MYT (UTC+8); late submissions will not be reviewed. Priority is given to candidates from the Global South and Global Majority communities, and community organizing, grassroots coordination, and self-taught practice are valued alongside formal credentials.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Global Artivism: Operations Fellow
item.description:
Organisation: Global Artivism (a fiscally sponsored project of Res Publica US Inc.)

Location: Remote, open globally. Priority given to applicants from the Global South.

Award: USD 1,000 to 1,500 per month (independent contractor fee), paid on receipt of monthly invoices. No employee benefits, paid leave, or insurance; contractor covers their own taxes and compliance.

Duration: 12-month independent contractor engagement from August 2026, with potential renewal subject to funding and mutual agreement. Flexible hours with scheduled calls across time zones.

Deadline: 16 Jul 2026

Global Artivism, a Global Majority-led movement that engages the power of arts and culture toward a just world on a safe planet, is contracting an Operations Fellow for a mentored, time-limited engagement focused on organizational infrastructure and systems design. Working alongside the Operations Director, the contractor designs operational frameworks spanning financial workflows, contractor management, governance alignment, and onboarding, contributing directly to infrastructure decisions while building practical experience in how a Global Majority-led movement organization functions operationally. The Fellow coordinates primarily with the Operations Director alongside the Governance Lead, Project Manager, Admin Coordinator, and Secretariat members (coordination relationships do not constitute supervisory authority; decision-making is distributed through governance structures and committees). KEY RESPONSIBILITIES: infrastructure design and framework development (designing and documenting operational frameworks; contributing to staffing models, organizational structures, and resource frameworks that enable both centralized and decentralized work; designing onboarding and operational guidance; aligning operational systems with governance frameworks and democratic decision-making); systems and knowledge documentation (operational guidance and toolkits for distributed teams; internal knowledge systems such as documentation, checklists, and decision trees); and capacity building and research (identifying where documentation or systems redesign enables teams to work more independently; flagging operational bottlenecks and proposing solutions; sharing learning with Council, committees, and teams; researching operational models relevant to Global Artivism's structure). REQUIRED: manages multiple tasks and meets deadlines with attention to detail and comfort coordinating logistics across a distributed team (experience via coordinating community events, grassroots logistics, or operations in any context counts alongside formal work history); good written English; comfort with digital tools (Google Workspace, Slack, or similar); genuine interest in operations, organizational systems, or movement infrastructure; ability to operate as an independent contractor; and commitment to Global Artivism's values. PREFERRED: background in business administration, project management, or finance; interest in international civil society, movement building, or Global Majority leadership; civil society/NGO/community experience; familiarity with digital project management or operations tools; multilingual ability in languages relevant to Global South contexts. This is an independent contractor engagement managed through Global Artivism's fiscal sponsorship with Res Publica US Inc.; the contractor sets their own hours and methods, may serve other clients, and handles their own taxes and local compliance. TO APPLY: submit an expression of interest via the ABM Intelligence application page; submissions are reviewed on a rolling basis by ABM's consulting team. Expressions of interest close Thursday 16 July 2026, 10pm MYT (UTC+8); late submissions will not be reviewed. Priority is given to candidates from the Global South and Global Majority communities, and community organizing, grassroots coordination, and self-taught practice are valued alongside formal credentials.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: IIAS: Asia in the World Fellowship Programme 2027-2028
item.description:
Organisation: International Institute for Asian Studies (IIAS), Leiden University

Location: Two phases: a partner institution in Asia, Africa, Latin America, or Europe (Phase 1), then Leiden, Netherlands (Phase 2). Open internationally.

Award: Leiden phase: EUR 1,250 per month stipend plus EUR 1,200 per month housing allowance, plus a travel grant; the Phase 1 stipend at the co-host institution is set by that institution. No application fee.

Duration: 9 months (two phases)

Deadline: 10 Sep 2026

The Asia in the World Fellowship Programme, run by the International Institute for Asian Studies (IIAS) at Leiden University, is a nine-month, two-phase fellowship pairing a residency at a partner institution in Asia, Africa, Latin America, or Europe (Phase 1) with six months in residence at IIAS in Leiden, the Netherlands (Phase 2). The programme supports interdisciplinary, humanities- and social-science-based research engaging Asia and its connections with the wider world. SUPPORT during the Leiden phase: EUR 1,250 per month stipend, EUR 1,200 per month housing allowance, and a travel grant; the Phase 1 stipend is provided by the co-host institution. ELIGIBILITY: scholars and researchers worldwide; open internationally. APPLICATIONS open 1 July 2026; deadline 10 September 2026. Apply via the IIAS fellowships page. NOTE: dates confirmed via secondary sources at time of entry (the funder page was not directly reachable); verify before applying.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Ucross General Residency (All Disciplines): Spring 2027
item.description:
Organisation: Ucross Foundation

Location: Sheridan, Wyoming, USA

Award: No charge for the residency; living accommodations, meals, and work space provided. USD 40 application fee.

Duration: Spring session (February through early June 2027); residencies range from two to six weeks

Deadline: 15 Jul 2026

Ucross offers fully funded artist residencies on a working cattle ranch in the foothills of Wyoming's Bighorn Mountains, providing uninterrupted time and space free from the pressures and distractions of daily life. Founded in 1981 with its first residency period in 1983, Ucross hosts approximately 115 individuals per year. Residencies range from two to six weeks; at any one time up to ten people are in residence, typically four writers, four visual artists, and two composers. Each artist receives living accommodations, meals, and work space at no charge. Lunches and dinners are prepared Monday to Friday by a professional chef (lunches delivered to studios, group dinners at 6 p.m.), with ample provisions for breakfasts and weekends; towels, linens, and weekly housekeeping are provided, and there is cell service and wireless internet throughout. Residents provide their own working materials and arrange their own travel to Sheridan, Wyoming. The program is open to visual artists, writers, composers, choreographers, interdisciplinary artists, performance artists, and collaborative teams; applicants must exhibit professional standing in their field, and both established and emerging artists are encouraged to apply. Applications are submitted by discipline category: dance, music/sound, performance, film/video, playwriting/screenwriting, visual art, fiction, nonfiction, poetry, or interdisciplinary. The work sample and project proposal are the most significant features of the application and should correspond to one another; if your work is in a single discipline, select the category that best fits your project, and apply under interdisciplinary only if the project genuinely combines multiple disciplines. Work-sample requirements by category: DANCE, 2-3 video files (each up to 8 minutes and 200 MB, or up to 3 Vimeo links, with a timestamp if longer than 8 minutes); MUSIC/SOUND, 3-5 audio files (each up to 8 minutes and 200 MB, or up to 3 Vimeo links; musical scores welcome); PERFORMANCE and FILM/VIDEO, 1-3 video files (each up to 5 minutes and under 200 MB, or up to 3 Vimeo/YouTube links, with a timestamp if longer than 5 minutes); PLAYWRITING/SCREENWRITING, one complete play double-spaced noting the 20-page section to read; VISUAL ART, 10 images at 4x6 inches and 300 dpi (JPG or TIF, no PDFs) plus a data sheet listing title, medium, size, and year; FICTION and NONFICTION, 20 pages of double-spaced writing; POETRY, 6-10 poems (10 maximum) in one PDF, each poem starting on a new page, total not exceeding 15 pages; INTERDISCIPLINARY, samples representing the genres you plan to work in, combining any of the above. All written work samples must be in English. There is a USD 40 application fee. The open call for the Spring 2027 session is open May 1 to July 15, 2026 (apply by 11:59 p.m. MT on July 15). Dedicated Fellowships for Native American Visual Artists and Writers and a separate Culinary Residency are also offered.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Ecoslay Mag: Open Call for Contributions, Issue 02 "The Urban Issue"
item.description:
Organisation: Ecoslay Mag

Location: Remote / international. Two online workshops with the editorial team; contributions delivered remotely.

Award: Financial compensation for selected contributions (amount unspecified). Editorial support, proofreading, and graphic design provided. No application fee.

Duration: Contributions developed approx. 1 September 2026 to 10 January 2027, with two online workshops (first around 15 September 2026). Final contribution deadline approx. 10 January 2027.

Deadline: 13 Jul 2026

Ecoslay Mag invites contributions for the second print issue of Ecoslay, "The Urban Issue," exploring the city as a space where intersectional ecological futures are negotiated, contested, resisted, and reinvented. In the Ecoslay way, pop culture and its influence are analysed and hijacked to communicate socio-ecological issues and ignite systemic change: what happens when climate politics meets internet culture, urban ecology meets reality TV, and sustainability discourse collides with camp, irony, humour, and memes. FORMATS (including but not limited to): essays, interviews, science communication, poems, visual art, documentation of (art)works, and fiction. TOPICS: urban climate communication through humour, memes, and pop culture; greenwashing, eco-branding, and sustainable aesthetics in urban contexts; urban farming, more-than-human life, housing crises, displacement and green gentrification, class and the city, maintenance and care work in relation to urbanism, urban environmental justice, and community organizing, and how these relate to pop culture; and reflections on what Ecoslay means in an age of overlapping crises. COMMITMENT: selected participants deliver one contribution to Issue 02 and attend two online workshops with the editorial team and fellow contributors for communal exchange and feedback on proposals and works-in-progress. WHAT'S OFFERED: financial compensation for selected contributions, editorial support, and edition, proofreading, and graphic design handled by the team. TIMELINE: open call closes 13 July 2026; selected contributors announced approx. 15 August 2026; work on contributions approx. 1 September 2026 to 10 January 2027; contributor workshop approx. 15 September 2026; final contribution deadline approx. 10 January 2027. HOW TO APPLY: submit via the Google form. Questions via ecoslay@protonmail.com or Instagram DM @ecoslay_mag.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Chornobyl40: Open Call 2026 (Virtual Residency & International Exhibition)
item.description:
Organisation: Chornobyl40 (gate22 / European Institute of Chornobyl consortium)

Location: Online residency (weekly sessions Tuesdays 14:00-18:00 CET); European touring exhibition 2027 in Madrid, Berlin, Toulouse, and Kyiv. Open internationally.

Award: Residency is free to attend. For the 20 projects selected for production: EUR 500 grant for fully digital works or EUR 1,000 grant for installations requiring physical materials, plus inclusion in the international exhibition. Travel, accommodation, and transport are not covered. No application fee.

Duration: Phase 1 virtual residency September-October 2026 (weekly Tuesday sessions, 30 hours total, minimum 90% attendance). Phase 2 selection and production. Phase 3 European touring exhibition in 2027.

Deadline: 12 Jul 2026

Chornobyl40 is a European artistic program, funded by the European Union, exploring the multiple legacies of the Chornobyl disaster not only as a past event but as an ongoing condition shaping the present and future. The open call invites artists, designers, researchers, collectives, and interdisciplinary/hybrid practitioners to engage with Chornobyl as a landscape, a memory, a political reality, and a speculative horizon. This is a call for interpretation, friction, and critical imagination, not illustration. STRUCTURE (three phases): PHASE 1, VIRTUAL RESIDENCY: 40 selected participants, September-October 2026, online, weekly sessions (Tuesdays 14:00-18:00 CET), 30 hours total with minimum 90% attendance; a structured process from idea to concept to pre-production including talks on Chornobyl, ecology, and contaminated territories, encounters with artists and technologists, a Concept Creation Hackathon, an XR Concept Development Workshop (exploratory, non-technical), a Mental Health & Eco-Anxiety Lab (4h), collective discussions, peer feedback, and tailored mentoring. PHASE 2, SELECTION & PRODUCTION: 20 projects selected (based on a video presentation, project documentation, and a live pitch) receive production support. PHASE 3, EXHIBITIONS: the 20 projects tour a European exhibition in 2027 (Madrid, Berlin, Toulouse, Kyiv); artist presence optional but encouraged. WHAT'S OFFERED: for the 40 participants, access to a curated residency, talks, workshops, mentoring, and a European network; for the 20 selected projects, a EUR 500 grant (fully digital works) or EUR 1,000 grant (installations needing physical materials) plus exhibition inclusion (travel, accommodation, and transport not covered). WHO CAN APPLY: artists, designers, researchers, collectives, and interdisciplinary/hybrid practices; open internationally, no age limit, emerging and established profiles equally encouraged. PROJECT THEMES: ecology and post-disaster environments, memory and trauma, geopolitical narratives, invisible forces (radiation, data, systems), technological imaginaries, nuclear energy, and misinformation and perception; projects must relate to Chornobyl (directly or indirectly), be adaptable to multiple exhibition contexts, and consider eco-responsibility; new and adapted existing works both welcome. SELECTION CRITERIA (equally weighted): artistic quality, relevance to the manifesto, eco-responsibility, digital dimension, and emotional and critical impact. TIMELINE: open call launched 19 May 2026; application deadline 12 July 2026; 40 participants announced end of July 2026. HOW TO APPLY: submit in English via the Google Form with a bio/CV link, portfolio link, and a project proposal (PDF, 2 A4 pages maximum). Application is free. Questions: contact@gate22.net.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: National Gallery of Iceland: CUSP Artist Residency (Reykjavik 2027)
item.description:
Organisation: National Gallery of Iceland (Listasafn Islands), CUSP project

Location: Reykjavik, Iceland. Residency hosted by the National Gallery of Iceland.

Award: Accommodation and working space for the residency; travel and subsistence stipend; an artist fee for collaborating in the open-lab/masterclass; and, depending on the artist's request and museum means, hardware and software for the working space. No application fee.

Duration: Up to four weeks, taking place in early 2027, with exact dates arranged with the selected artist.

Deadline: 03 Sep 2026

The National Gallery of Iceland announces an artist residency open call under its CUSP program, a European cooperation project funded through Creative Europe's Culture grant. Artists working in complex media or emerging technologies (e.g. generative AI, custom sensors, sound, software-based, blockchain, physical and digital hybrid) are invited to apply. This is the first of four residency stays offered to different artists through the program, one in each of the participating countries (Iceland, Norway, Czechia, and Ukraine), followed by two micro residencies in the Netherlands and Iceland. The programme brings together artists working in time-based media with museum professionals, conservators, and technology specialists developing preservation and conservation methodologies to ensure the long-term endurance of artworks. The selected artist will meet with a preservation specialist or conservator specializing in time-based media art for an in-depth artist interview addressing the preservation needs of their medium, and will present their body of work and working methods publicly during an open-lab masterclass designed in collaboration with the museum. RESIDENCY: up to four weeks in early 2027 in Reykjavik, exact dates arranged with the selected artist. WHAT IS PROVIDED: accommodation and working space; a travel and subsistence stipend; an artist fee for the open lab/masterclass; and possibly hardware and software depending on the artist's request and museum means. ELIGIBILITY: international artists based outside Iceland; applicants must be residents of any of the 27 EU member states or of non-EU countries with association agreements (EFTA/EEA members, acceding countries, candidate countries, and European Neighbourhood Policy countries). APPLICATION: complete the application form with full name, country of residence, gender identity, contact information, an artistic statement (max 2500 characters), a motivation letter (max 2500 characters), a draft idea of the public workshop or masterclass (max 1000 characters), links to social media/professional websites, and a CV and portfolio upload (max 10MB each). DEADLINE: 3 September 2026 at 23:59 GMT. ABOUT CUSP: Creative Understanding, Saving and Preserving Time-Based Media Art is a transnational cooperation project co-funded by the EU's Creative Europe Culture grant, led by the National Gallery of Iceland with Nasjonalmuseet (Norway), LI-MA (Netherlands), Vasulka Kitchen (Czechia), and Mystetskyi Art Arsenal (Ukraine), focused on preserving and presenting time-based media art. Inquiries: cusp.residency.is [at] gmail.com.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Connections Through Culture Grants 2026 (British Council)
item.description:
Organisation: British Council

Location: International collaboration grant. Each application must involve at least one UK-based partner and at least one partner based in an eligible participating country; each partner must be legally based in the country they apply from throughout the project.

Award: Grants of between GBP 5,000 and GBP 15,000. Free to apply.

Duration: Project-based; develop and deliver collaborative projects across a range of themes and art forms.

Deadline: 12 Aug 2026

Connections Through Culture (CTC) grants are designed to enable meaningful partnerships, cross-cultural exchange and long-term collaboration between the UK and participating countries. In 2026, CTC incorporates the grants programmes formerly known as Biennials Connect (Visual Arts) and Architecture, Design and Fashion Biennials and Festivals Grants, and serves as the British Council's major Arts grants programme for 2026 (in place of International Collaboration Grants). It offers grants of between GBP 5,000 and GBP 15,000 to support creative practitioners, cultural professionals, artists, organisations, festivals, biennials, hubs and collectives to develop and deliver collaborative projects across a wide range of themes and art forms, including applied arts, architecture, curating, dance, design, digital, drawing, education, fashion, film, installation, interdisciplinary work, painting, performance, photography, printmaking, public art, research, sculpture, social practice, sound art, textiles, video, visual arts and writing. ELIGIBILITY: applications must involve collaboration between at least one UK-based partner and at least one partner based in an eligible participating country; each partner must be legally based in the country they are applying from at the time of application and throughout the project activity period. PARTICIPATING COUNTRIES: Albania, Argentina, Australia, Bangladesh, Bosnia and Herzegovina, Colombia, Mainland China, Egypt, Indonesia, Kazakhstan, Kenya, Kosovo, Lebanon, Malaysia, Mexico, Montenegro, New Zealand, Nigeria, North Macedonia, Pakistan, Palestine, Peru, Philippines, Saudi Arabia, Serbia, South Africa, Tanzania, Thailand, Tunisia, Turkey, United Arab Emirates, UK, Ukraine, Uzbekistan, Venezuela, and Vietnam. Eligible applicants include artists and creative practitioners, arts and cultural organisations, festivals and biennials, cultural professionals, creative hubs/networks/collectives, and organisations working across arts, culture and the creative sectors. Note: applications involving projects organised by or taking place as part of Visual Arts, Architecture, Design, Fashion and Craft festivals should be made by the festival or biennial organisation rather than by individual artists, designers or practitioners. No application fee. Deadline 12 August 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: RAMDAM, un centre d'art: Extended Possibilities Residency 2027
item.description:
Organisation: RAMDAM, un centre d'art

Location: Sainte-Foy-les-Lyon (near Lyon), France; open to artists legally resident in Culture Moves Europe eligible countries (EU member states, outermost regions, overseas countries and territories, and some non-EU countries); artists from mainland (hexagonal) France cannot apply

Award: EUR 1,350 per resident allowance for subsistence and other costs, plus travel allowance of EUR 400 (under 5,000 km) or EUR 800 (from 5,000 km). Top-ups: green mobility EUR 400 per resident (600 km or more by non-plane transport), visa EUR 120, outermost regions/OCT EUR 175, family EUR 200 per child under 18. On-site accommodation and technical facilities provided. Supported by Culture Moves Europe.

Duration: 45 days, 1 February to 15 March 2027

Deadline: 14 Jul 2026

Extended Possibilities gives artists the opportunity to work on site at RAMDAM, un centre d'art for 45 days, a long period allowing them to benefit from technical support and to present ongoing work to the public. Artists receive on-site accommodation (a collective room for the whole 45 days), access to a well-maintained, up-to-date technical facility, a grant to support their work, and a lump-sum allowance for travel and visas. RAMDAM is an activist space supporting artists in their artistic research; residencies are open to artists or teams engaged in creation, research, or experimentation, with no obligation to produce a finished work: dance, theatre, performance, painting, sculpture, drawing, film, video, literary writing, screenwriting, digital art, and hybrid forms are all welcome. The call focuses on multidisciplinary projects and research that break down barriers between disciplines and challenge their own field of practice, and considerable attention is given to how the work fits into a social and/or political context and resonates with our times. THE CALL SUPPORTS: 1 or 2 artistic teams (RAMDAM can host at most 5 artists); performing artists of all disciplines; projects at early stages of research-creation and/or more advanced (residencies can be writing residencies and/or stage residencies). All artists must be available for the full 45 days from 1 February to 15 March 2027 and must be from eligible countries (EU countries, outermost regions, overseas countries and territories, and some non-EU countries); artists from mainland (hexagonal) France cannot apply. The working and living spaces are wheelchair accessible, though reaching RAMDAM by public transport can be difficult for disabled people; accessibility support is available via Culture Moves Europe for residents with disabilities. ALLOWANCES: EUR 1,350 per resident for subsistence; travel allowance EUR 400 under 5,000 km or EUR 800 from 5,000 km (travel under 600 km must be by green means of transport unless disability or island residence requires otherwise); green mobility top-up EUR 400 per resident for non-plane travel over 600 km; visa top-up EUR 120; outermost regions/OCT top-up EUR 175; family top-up EUR 200 per child under 18 (housing capacity is limited). TO APPLY: applications in English or French; download the residency project and administrative form documents and email the complete application to contact@ramdamcda.org with subject line 'extended possibilities - 2027' (no WeTransfer, no important information in the email body; reduce file sizes; a third document may be added to help understand the work). Confirmation within 7 working days; shortlisted applicants have video calls with the residency committee in mid-August. Deadline: Tuesday 14 July 2026, 11:59 p.m. NOTE: the call is arbitrarily limited to the first 40 applications received (still open as of 1 July 2026), so apply early. Made possible by the European programme Culture Moves Europe.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Creative Steps for Individuals 2026 (Arts Council of Wales)
item.description:
Organisation: Arts Council of Wales

Location: Wales, United Kingdom. For artists and creatives based in Wales.

Award: GBP 500 to GBP 7,500 (plus access costs). Applicants can apply for up to 90% of the developmental cost; the remaining 10% must come from a non-Arts Council of Wales / non-National Lottery source (in-kind, cash, or both).

Duration: Supports a stage of professional development; allow a minimum of 10 working weeks between the deadline and the project start date.

Deadline: 29 Jul 2026

Creative Steps funds the professional, business and organisational development of artists who have experienced barriers to careers within the arts. Creative Steps for Individuals is open to individual artists and creatives who identify as being Ethnically and Culturally diverse, D/deaf, disabled or neurodivergent, groups that are underrepresented in Arts Council of Wales funding programmes due to barriers to entry. Applicants must have demonstrable experience as an artist or creative professional; those without may still be supported on the basis of artistic potential by submitting a portfolio, CV or examples of work, discussed with a Relationship Manager. WHAT IT SUPPORTS: career development, NOT creating new work or project delivery. Examples include peer-to-peer learning, training courses (not formal/vocational education), building networks, mentorship/coaching, business development/social media/marketing advice, website development/marketing materials/equipment or materials, and setting up a new organisation. Budgets may include the applicant's own time, freelancer costs (paid at standard industry rates), mentor/consultant costs, access costs, and childcare/caregiving costs. AMOUNT: GBP 500 to GBP 7,500 (plus access costs); up to 90% of the developmental cost, with the remaining 10% from a non-Arts Council of Wales / non-National Lottery source (in-kind, cash, or both, confirmed by letters of support). No application fee. APPLY: applicants must express interest at least 3 weeks before the deadline to receive support from an Application Coach or Access Support coordinator; an application form is provided by a Relationship Manager. The fund runs in rounds; the next round (Round 2 2026) has an Expression of Interest cut-off of 8 July 2026 and an application deadline of 29 July 2026. Later rounds: Round 3 (EOI 30 September, deadline 21 October 2026) and Round 4 (EOI 10 February, deadline 3 March 2027).

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: SIAP: Project Funding for Individual Artists 2026 (Arts Council NI)
item.description:
Organisation: Arts Council of Northern Ireland

Location: Northern Ireland, United Kingdom. Applicants must be based in Northern Ireland.

Award: GBP 62 to GBP 15,000. Funded through the National Lottery Support for Individual Artists Programme (SIAP).

Duration: Project-based activity; duration not specified.

Deadline: 30 Jul 2026

Project Funding for Individuals, part of Arts Council NI's National Lottery Support for Individual Artists Programme (SIAP), funds project-based creative activities across all artistic disciplines. Eligible activities include creating new work, research and development, residencies, mentoring, professional development, and time dedicated to specific creative outcomes. ELIGIBLE COSTS: art materials, artist fees/time, residency costs, travel (mileage at GBP 0.257/mile), venue hire, technical equipment (max 50% of request value), and IT equipment (laptops/PCs up to GBP 1,200; tablets up to GBP 500). ELIGIBILITY: artists of all disciplines and career stages, including emerging artists (less than 5 years professional experience), and in some cases technical staff/administrators; students and academics at third-level institutions may apply with departmental approval showing the costs are not covered by their employer or academic work. AMOUNT: GBP 62 to GBP 15,000. No application fee. Deadline: Thursday 30 July 2026, 12:00 noon (online submission required).

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: SIAP: Artists' International Development Fund 2026 (Arts Council NI)
item.description:
Organisation: Arts Council of Northern Ireland

Location: Northern Ireland, United Kingdom. Applicants must be based in Northern Ireland; international development takes place abroad with overseas partners/hosts.

Award: GBP 62 to GBP 15,000. Funded through the National Lottery Support for Individual Artists Programme (SIAP).

Duration: Early-stage international development opportunities; residencies supported for a maximum of 4 weeks.

Deadline: 30 Jul 2026

The Artists' International Development Fund (AIDF), part of Arts Council NI's National Lottery Support for Individual Artists Programme (SIAP), funds early-stage international development opportunities for individual, freelance and self-employed artists to build networks abroad. ELIGIBLE COSTS: art materials, travel, residencies (up to 4 weeks), technical fees, venue hire, and equipment, with IT devices capped at GBP 1,200 for laptops/PCs and GBP 500 for tablets. ELIGIBILITY: artists across all disciplines, particularly emerging artists (under 5 years professional experience); academics and students at third-level institutions may apply if the project differs from their employment/studies. AIDF applicants must have received recognition for their work in Northern Ireland, lack extensive international experience, and demonstrate interest in their work from an overseas partner/host. AMOUNT: GBP 62 to GBP 15,000. No application fee. Deadline: Thursday 30 July 2026, 12:00 noon.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Jentel Artist Residency
item.description:
Organisation: Jentel Foundation

Location: Banner, Wyoming, USA

Award: USD 100 weekly stipend (about USD 300 total over three weeks), plus private bedroom, separate studio space, and shared living and kitchen facilities provided at no cost USD 30 application fee

Duration: Approximately 3 weeks (arrive on the 15th, depart the morning of the 7th of the following month)

Deadline: 15 Sep 2026

The Jentel Artist Residency offers approximately three-week residencies in the foothills of the Bighorn Mountains near Banner, Wyoming, giving artists, writers, and composers dedicated time for creative work. Sessions run from mid-January through early December, hosting six residents at a time. It is open to visual artists in any medium, writers of fiction, creative nonfiction, or poetry, and composers across musical genres. Residents receive a private bedroom, a separate studio, shared living and kitchen space, and a USD 100 weekly stipend to help cover food and personal expenses. Applicants must be at least 25 years old and be U.S. citizens or foreign nationals whose primary residence is in the U.S. during the application year, and may not be enrolled full-time in a degree program. There is a USD 30 non-refundable application fee, with deadlines of September 15 for Winter and Spring sessions and January 15 for Summer and Fall sessions.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: SIAP: Major Individual Artists Award 2026/27 (Arts Council NI)
item.description:
Organisation: Arts Council of Northern Ireland

Location: Northern Ireland, United Kingdom. Applicants must be based in Northern Ireland.

Award: Up to GBP 15,000 per award; four awards available in 2026/27. Funded through the National Lottery Support for Individual Artists Programme (SIAP).

Duration: Supports specific projects, specialised research, personal artistic development and materials/equipment. Activity duration not specified.

Deadline: 20 Jul 2026

The Major Individual Artists award, part of Arts Council NI's National Lottery Support for Individual Artists Programme (SIAP), funds extended or ambitious work by established artists, covering specific projects, specialised research, personal artistic development, and materials/equipment. AMOUNT: four awards of up to GBP 15,000 each in 2026/27. ELIGIBILITY: established artists across all disciplines with national/international recognition who have not previously received a major award; academics and postgraduate artists are eligible if the project costs do not relate to their institutional work or studies. Applicants must contact the relevant artform officer before submission (by 10 July 2026); applications without this prior contact are ineligible. No application fee. The online application system closes precisely at the deadline and applicants must receive a confirmation email to verify successful submission. Deadline: Monday 20 July 2026, 12:00 noon.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Canon Foundation in Europe Research Fellowships 2026 (Europe–Japan Exchange)
item.description:
Organisation: Canon Foundation in Europe

Location: Europe or Japan (bilateral exchange; fellows arrange own host institution)

Award: Up to EUR 30,000 per year (pro-rated for shorter stays). No application fee.

Duration: Minimum 3 months, maximum 1 year. Fellowships for 2027 start.

Deadline: 15 Sep 2026

Canon Foundation in Europe Research Fellowships support bilateral research exchange between Europe and Japan, awarding up to 15 fellowships annually. AMOUNT: up to EUR 30,000 per year (pro-rated for shorter stays), flexible — covers living costs, travel, insurance, research expenses, and books; fellows determine their own allocation. ELIGIBILITY: European nationals (including UK, Israel, Switzerland, Turkey, Balkan and Baltic countries) conducting research in Japan; or Japanese nationals conducting research in Europe. Applicants must have completed at least a Master's or PhD degree within the last 10 years (older qualifications may apply with supporting documentation). No restrictions on research field. Priority given to first-time applicants to Europe/Japan. Fellows arrange their own host institution and advisor. Up to 15 fellowships awarded annually. Deadline: 15 September 2026, 23:59 CET (for fellowships starting January-December 2027). Apply at https://portal.canonfoundation.org/. Contact: canon.foundation@canon-europe.com.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Fulbright-Iméra Mediterranean Studies Award 2027-2028 (Aix-Marseille, US Faculty)
item.description:
Organisation: Iméra – Aix Marseille Université, in partnership with the Fulbright Commission

Location: Aix-Marseille, France (campus residency)

Award: €2,400 (~USD 2,600) monthly stipend; fully covered accommodation (valued USD 788–1,966/month); €1,200 (~USD 1,300) travel allowance. No application fee.

Duration: 5 or 10 months (September 2027 – June 2028, flexible start/end dates).

Deadline: 15 Sep 2026

The Fulbright-Iméra Mediterranean Studies Award supports US faculty to conduct research at the Iméra Institute for Advanced Study at Aix Marseille Université. ELIGIBILITY: mid-career, senior, or community college faculty affiliated with a US institution of higher education; US citizens residing outside France (permanent residents of France are ineligible); any discipline relating to Mediterranean studies; English sufficient (French desirable). 1 or 2 grants awarded. PROVIDED: €2,400/month stipend; fully equipped apartment with linens; office in co-working space; visa fees waived; academic event organisation expenses covered; school placement assistance for dependents. DURATION: 5 or 10 months (September 2027 – June 2028, flexible). DEADLINE: 15 September 2026. Apply via the Fulbright Scholar Program at https://fulbrightscholars.org/award/fulbright-imera-mediterranean-studies-award-aix-marseille-university.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Kometa Fellows 2026: Field Journalism, Photography, and Writing
item.description:
Organisation: Kometa (French literary and journalism magazine)

Location: Remote / field-based (international)

Award: €10,000 per Fellow, six Fellows selected. No application fee.

Duration: One year of funding for field work and research

Deadline: 31 Jul 2026

Kometa, a French literary and journalism magazine, awards fellowships to journalists, writers, and photographers documenting reality in contexts of war, censorship, or severe social and political tensions. Projects must be rooted in direct field experience and focused on contemporary rather than historical events. Projects already under a contractual agreement are ineligible. AMOUNT: €10,000 per Fellow, six Fellows selected. WHAT FELLOWS RECEIVE: one year of funding; publication of the completed project (partial or full) in Kometa; opportunity to contribute to the monthly newsletter; mentorship through editorial collaboration. 2026 FOCUS AREAS: disinformation and narrative wars; political and social changes in the Gulf. DEADLINE: 31 July 2026. APPLY via the Tally form (https://tally.so/r/J9XxMK), in French or English (about 30 to 45 minutes). Contact: fellows@kometarevue.com.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Híbrides: Small Embodied Data - Call for Contributions (2nd Edition)
item.description:
Organisation: Axolot.cat collective and DARTS research group, Universitat Oberta de Catalunya (UOC)

Location: Barcelona, Spain; UOC Poblenou campus and Convent de Sant Agustí. In-person attendance required.

Award: Oral presentations: €100 honorarium (invoice required). Performances: €400 honorarium (invoice required). No submission fee.

Duration: Festival runs 26-28 November 2026.

Deadline: 07 Sep 2026

Híbrides is a biennial of artistic research co-organised by the Axolot.cat collective and the DARTS (Design, Art, Technology and Society) research group at the UOC in Barcelona. The second edition, "Small Embodied Data," centres on the concept of classification -- approached through machine learning, data science, algorithmic systems, and questions of who designs classifiers, under what ethical codes, and to what end. The biennial brings classification into dialogue with its previous themes of Small Data (community-defined, intentional data collection and analysis) and Embodiment (bodily experience as a foundation of situated, sensory knowledge). Both academic and non-academic contributions are welcome. FORMATS ACCEPTED: Oral Presentations (20 minutes; artistic practice, artistic research, or academic research projects; may be video-recorded for a public archive and developed into short papers); Performances (20-30 minutes; must engage with the physical body, stage, and technology; complete works, including previously presented work; takes place in a multipurpose space with technical support). SUBMISSION REQUIREMENTS: online form with abstract (max 1,000 characters), professional biography (max 1,000 characters), and link to representative materials (3-5 images; video and technical rider required for performances). HONORARIA: oral presentations €100; performances €400; invoice required in both cases. Participants must follow the event's code of conduct. Event runs 26-28 November 2026 at UOC Poblenou campus and Convent de Sant Agustí, Barcelona. Deadline: 7 September 2026. Submit at https://hibrides.axolot.cat/opencall.html.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Democracy Stories Lab: Open Call for Visual Storytellers
item.description:
Organisation: People Powered and Democracy Narratives Alliance

Location: Worldwide; remote. Global screening event for winners.

Award: USD $1,000 for each of 4 winners; USD $500 for each of 5 honorable mentions. Plus amplification across People Powered and Democracy Narratives Alliance networks and feature in a global screening event. No application fee.

Duration: One-time award.

Deadline: 14 Sep 2026

The Democracy Stories Lab, an initiative of People Powered (a global hub for participatory democracy) in partnership with the Democracy Narratives Alliance, invites creators worldwide to submit visual content -- short videos, illustrations, or graphics -- that tells a compelling story about democracy, voice, fairness, belonging, and community power. Content can be original or already published, as long as it is the applicant's own work and engages with narrative frameworks from the Democracy Narratives Alliance's research on 'How to Talk About Democracy.' Tone is open; serious and comedic approaches are both welcome. AWARDS: 4 winning entries receive USD $1,000 each; 5 honorable mentions receive USD $500 each; all winners receive amplification across People Powered and Democracy Narratives Alliance communications networks and are featured in a global screening event. WHO CAN APPLY: individual creators and small teams anywhere in the world; especially welcomes creators who reach audiences outside usual democracy circles, who come from communities traditionally with less of a say in public discourse, and who work in civic tech, advocacy, community organising, comedy, art, illustration, games, or lifestyle content. Applications open 14 July 2026 via the Democracy Stories Lab page at https://www.peoplepowered.org/news-content/announcing-the-democracy-stories-lab; subscribe to the People Powered newsletter to be notified when the form opens. Submission deadline: 14 September 2026. Winners announced October-November 2026. Note: the initiative was itself voted into existence through People Powered's participatory budgeting process (PP Decides).

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Revitalizing Rituals 2026 Open Call
item.description:
Organisation: Homo Faber Alapítvány (Homo Faber Foundation)

Location: Bhar Lazreg, Tunisia and Somogy County, Hungary; multi-stage in-person residency across both sites.

Award: EUR 6,000 gross artist fee and production budget; international travel and per diems covered; on-site accommodation provided. No application fee.

Duration: Multi-stage residency: Placemaking Camp (1 week, November 2026, Hungary); Storytellers Camp (1 week, March 2027, Tunisia); Residency Period (minimum 1 month, April-July 2027); public presentation (August 2027); Closing Event (3 days, winter/early spring 2028, Hungary).

Deadline: 19 Jul 2026

Revitalizing Rituals invites artists from all disciplines -- sound, movement, visual arts, installation, performance, and transdisciplinary practices -- to join a research-creation journey across two landscapes: Bhar Lazreg in Tunisia and the wetlands of Somogy County in Hungary. The project explores rituals as living forms of belonging: reconnecting communities, land, memory, food, ecological reciprocity, and the more-than-human world. Selected artists work with local communities as co-participants in a shared inquiry into moving from extraction toward reciprocity. Organised by Homo Faber Alapítvány (Hungary). AMOUNT: EUR 6,000 gross artist fee and production budget; international travel and per diems covered; on-site accommodation provided. ELIGIBILITY: minimum 5 years of active artistic practice; portfolio reflecting interest in situated or relational work; no formal degree required; working language is English (local language navigation valued); must be available for the full residency cycle including all international travel; must be able to legally receive the fee and invoice the organising partners from country of residence. Strongly encourages applications from historically marginalised or underrepresented voices. KEY DATES: Application Deadline 19 July 2026 (midnight) -- note the open call PDF also references a 5 July date; the officially promoted deadline is 19 July; Online Q&A 29 June 2026, 14:00 Tunisia / 15:00 CET (register at https://forms.gle/brnZvXvFe4JB8zL26); Online Conversations with shortlisted artists: mid July to mid August 2026; Selection Results: 31 August 2026; Placemaking Camp: 1 week, end of November 2026, Hungary; Storytellers Camp: 1 week, March 2027, Tunisia; Residency Period: minimum 1 month (splittable), April to July 2027; Final Presentation: August 2027; Closing Event: 3 days, winter/early spring 2028, Hungary. Apply via the open call form linked at https://homofaber.hu/wp-content/uploads/2026/06/Artist-Open-Call-FINAL-in-the-RR-form.pdf.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Jamverse Jam (Protocolized worldbuilding contest)
item.description:
Organisation: Protocolized (Summer of Protocols)

Location: Online / Worldwide

Award: One USD 1,000 grand prize plus at least 10 additional USD 200 prizes; winning artifacts are published on the Jamverse website. No entry fee.

Duration: One-off contest; submit up to three artifacts by 31 July 2026

Deadline: 31 Jul 2026

Protocolized's fourth contest invites artists and writers (storytellers, poets, comic artists) to build, extend, and connect the Jamverse, a shared near-future science fiction universe of four intersecting story cycles (The Stockton Chronicles, Legends and Ledgers, UET-Trainverse, and Zoothesia). ELIGIBILITY: open to anyone; you may submit up to three in-universe worldbuilding artifacts, and the same creator can win multiple prizes. REQUIREMENTS: each artifact must be under 1,000 words (200-700 is the sweet spot) or a visual equivalent (comic panel, poster, advertisement, etc.) and must include at least one element drawn from one of the four sub-world 'toy boxes', a surprising 'traffic jam' (an emergent, ah-ha consequence of a technology or protocol), and a relation to the theme of Stigmergy (indirect coordination through modifying a shared environment). Artifact types can include comic panels, job postings, poems, forum threads, historic documents, or encyclopedia entries. APPLY: submit each artifact as a public Google Doc via the contest form, including the artifact, a one-paragraph explanation of the worlds/toy-box components used and how the piece relates to stigmergy and explores a traffic jam, and, if AI was used, documentation of your AI/LLM protocol. PRIZES: one USD 1,000 grand prize and at least 10 USD 200 prizes, all published on the Jamverse website. DEADLINE: 31 July 2026. Contest rules, the current Jamverse stories, and starter materials are on the Jamverse website.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Restless Egg: Batch 2, Luxury Technology (Accelerator for Artist-Founders) 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Restless Egg

Location: London-based incubator (UK); open to creative "artist-founders" internationally, with in-person gatherings (a first-batch salon was held at Sybil in Berlin).

Award: Equity-based investment (NOT a non-dilutive grant): USD 100,000 initial investment for 5% equity, plus up to USD 175,000 in follow-on investment when predefined performance metrics are met. Includes a high-touch accelerator program and access to a network of subject-matter experts, collaborators, operators and investors.

Duration: Six-month accelerator program

Deadline: 17 Jul 2026

Restless Egg is a London-based incubator for "artist-founders", an emerging genre of creative people whose work sits between art, technology and building commercial products. It invests in creative founders and runs a six-month, high-touch accelerator program to help them build commercially successful and scalable companies at the bleeding edge of culture and technology. BATCH 2 (LUXURY TECHNOLOGY) is open for applications, seeking founders building luxury technologies: commercial products that use AI and other emerging technologies to unlock richer, stranger, more meaningful forms of human experience, not just more efficiency. For this batch Restless Egg has doubled its initial investment to USD 100,000 for 5% equity, and increased its follow-on investment to up to USD 175,000, aiming to give founders more runway, more support and more room to build the most ambitious companies. The program supports rapid learning, sharper product and narrative development, and real investability through close engagement with Restless Egg and its network of subject-matter experts, collaborators, operators and investors; the follow-on is released when predefined performance metrics are met. NOTE: this is a dilutive equity investment (it takes a 5% stake), not a non-dilutive grant or fellowship. Applications are open (no fixed deadline stated). Apply at https://apply.restlessegg.com.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Social Shifters Global Innovation Challenge 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Social Shifters

Location: Global; open to youth-led ventures from anywhere in the world.

Award: Multiple awards of up to USD 15,000 each (reported grants from USD 3,000 to USD 15,000), plus pitch coaching, a finalist showcase, free founder support from registration, and ongoing access to fellowships and paid work through the Shifters 100 alumni network that corporate partners recruit from. Free to enter.

Duration: Submissions close 31 August 2026; finalists announced October, winners December 2026

Deadline: 31 Aug 2026

Social Shifters, a Scottish-registered charity backed by corporate partners, runs its Global Innovation Challenge as a funnel into a longer founder-development pipeline rather than a one-off prize. ELIGIBILITY: founders aged 18 to 30 at submission, leading a fully youth-led venture that is already live and producing measurable social or environmental impact, aligned to at least one Sustainable Development Goal; concept-stage ideas and student projects do not qualify. Multiple winners receive up to USD 15,000 each, plus pitch coaching, a finalist showcase, and ongoing access to fellowships and paid work through the Shifters 100 alumni network. Free coaching is available from the moment you register, so the payoff to registering early is high. Submissions close 31 August 2026 (5pm UTC), with finalists announced in October and winners in December 2026. Apply at https://www.socialshifters.co/global-innovation-challenge/.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Expo Bart: OPEN HALL 2026 (Free Project Space, Technique & Support)
item.description:
Organisation: Expo Bart

Location: Expo Bart, NYMA, Waalplein 6, Nijmegen, Netherlands

Award: No fee and no rent, but also no artist fee or other compensation (geen vergoeding). Provides free use of a 100 m2 fully blackout-able project space, limited technique (2 short-throw beamers, a PA set with mixer and microphones, and light spots) and helping hands, plus promotion of your event via Expo Bart's channels.

Duration: Programme runs 13 November to 20 December 2026; book one or more days (open Thursday to Sunday, 12:00-17:00; other times negotiable)

Deadline: 12 Jul 2026

Do you have an idea you have always wanted to carry out but can never find the space for? Expo Bart in Nijmegen invites you to apply for OPEN HALL. From 13 November to 20 December 2026, Expo Bart makes its project space, technique and helping hands available to help realise your idea, whether that is a week-long exhibition, a one-day workshop, a film shoot, a knitting club, a concert series, a film night, a performance, a dinner, a combination of all of these, or something completely different. PRACTICAL: you can sign up for one or more days between 13 November and 20 December 2026; Expo Bart is open Thursday to Sunday from 12:00 to 17:00 (organising something outside opening hours can be discussed together); the 100 m2 project space is fully blackout-able; limited technique is available, namely two short-throw beamers, a PA set (with mixer and microphones) and light spots; and your event is of course shared via Expo Bart's channels. There is no compensation and no rent is charged. APPLY by sending your idea (max 1 A4, including photos) to info@expobart.nl with the subject line 'OPEN HALL', stating your plan/idea, your preferred day(s), and what you need in terms of production. Deadline: 12 July 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Stimuleringsfonds: Digital Culture Grant Scheme 2026 (final round)
item.description:
Organisation: Stimuleringsfonds Creatieve Industrie (Creative Industries Fund NL)

Location: Netherlands. Requires Dutch Chamber of Commerce (KvK) registration.

Award: EUR 10,000-25,000 for solo projects; EUR 10,000-50,000 for collaborative projects with partners. At least 20 percent of total project costs must be covered by co-financing. No application fee.

Duration: Project-based.

Deadline: 19 Aug 2026

The Stimuleringsfonds Digital Culture Grant Scheme (Deelregeling Digitale cultuur) supports projects by makers, researchers, design studios and cultural institutions positioned professionally in digital culture. Solo projects can receive EUR 10,000-25,000; collaborative projects with partners can receive up to EUR 50,000. At least 20 percent of total project costs must be covered by co-financing. Applicants must be registered with the Dutch Chamber of Commerce (KvK). The 2026 application window for this round runs 12-19 August 2026 (opens 12 August 15:00, closes 19 August 16:00 CEST); in 2026 the scheme runs only two rounds and this is the last. No application fee. Apply via the official scheme page.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Stimuleringsfonds + Film Fund: Immerse\Interact 2026 (second round)
item.description:
Organisation: Stimuleringsfonds Creatieve Industrie and the Netherlands Film Fund

Location: Netherlands. Requires Dutch Chamber of Commerce (KvK) registration.

Award: Development: EUR 15,000 for individual makers (plus up to EUR 5,000 coaching, max EUR 20,000) or EUR 25,000 for producers (plus up to EUR 5,000 coaching). Realisation: up to EUR 35,000 for individual makers or up to EUR 125,000 for producers (Fund covers max 85 percent). No application fee.

Duration: Project-based, development and realisation phases.

Deadline: 25 Aug 2026

Immerse\Interact is a joint grant scheme of the Stimuleringsfonds Creatieve Industrie and the Netherlands Film Fund for immersive and interactive media projects (VR, MR, AR and other interactive storytelling). It funds both development and realisation. Development support is EUR 15,000 for individual makers (plus up to EUR 5,000 coaching, capped at EUR 20,000) or EUR 25,000 for producers; realisation support is up to EUR 35,000 for individual makers or up to EUR 125,000 for producers (the Fund covers a maximum of 85 percent of costs). Open to professional makers, collectives and producers with a demonstrable track record in immersive or interactive media; applicants must be registered with the Dutch Chamber of Commerce (KvK). The second 2026 round runs 24 June to 25 August 2026 (opens 24 June 15:00, closes 25 August 16:00 CEST). No application fee. Questions: immerseinteract@stimuleringsfonds.nl.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Harvard Radcliffe Institute: Fellowship Program 2027-2028
item.description:
Organisation: Harvard Radcliffe Institute

Location: Cambridge, Massachusetts, USA; fellows must relocate to and reside in the Cambridge/Boston area for the full fellowship (September through May).

Award: USD 78,000 stipend plus an additional USD 5,000 for project expenses. Relocation, housing, and childcare funds may also be provided. No application fee.

Duration: One academic year, September 2027 through May 2028 (residential)

Deadline: 10 Sep 2026

The Harvard Radcliffe Institute Fellowship offers scientists, writers, scholars, public intellectuals, and artists a year to pursue ambitious projects in a vibrant interdisciplinary setting amid the resources of Harvard University. The Institute welcomes innovative work that confronts pressing social, scientific, and policy issues and seeks to engage audiences beyond academia. Reflecting Radcliffe's history, it welcomes (but does not limit eligibility to) proposals focused on women, gender, and society, or that draw on the Schlesinger Library's collections. It also invites proposals relevant to the Institute's 2024-2029 focus area, academic freedom and connecting across difference (intellectual virtues, free and open inquiry, diversity of thought, political polarization, peace and conflict, inequality, religious pluralism, and related higher-education policy issues), including work that constructively challenges disciplinary orthodoxies or advances transformative perspectives. SUPPORT: USD 78,000 stipend plus USD 5,000 for project expenses, with relocation, housing, and childcare funds available; fellows must reside in the Cambridge/Boston area from September through May. ELIGIBILITY: open to individuals across career stages; tenure is not required and applicants need not be academics. This is NOT a postdoctoral fellowship; those currently enrolled in a degree program are ineligible, as are former Harvard Radcliffe fellows (1999-present). Applicants must meet discipline-specific criteria. APPLY: register on the online portal and select an area (Humanities and Social Sciences; Creative Arts; Nonfiction and Journalism; or Science, Engineering, and Mathematics). Materials: application form, CV, 1,400-word project proposal (with bibliography when appropriate), a writing or work sample, and three references. DEADLINES: humanities, social sciences, creative arts, and nonfiction and journalism by 10 September 2026, 5pm ET; science, engineering, and mathematics by 1 October 2026, 5pm ET.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: King Baudouin Foundation: Ernest Solvay Fund 2026 (Call for Projects)
item.description:
Organisation: Fonds Ernest Solvay, managed with the support of the King Baudouin Foundation

Location: Organisations implementing a project within a 100 km radius of a Solvay industrial, R&I or administrative site

Award: Up to EUR 10,000 per initiative. Funding covers only expenses incurred after the results are announced (mid-December 2026); already-incurred expenses are not reimbursed. No application fee.

Duration: Project implemented after mid-December 2026 announcement

Deadline: 30 Sep 2026

The Ernest Solvay Fund provides financial support for local-impact initiatives in three areas: scientific education (science, technology, engineering and mathematics), planet progress (environmental initiatives) and better life (community well-being). ELIGIBILITY: organisations submitting a project must implement it within a 100 km radius of a Solvay industrial, R&I or administrative site. FUNDING: up to EUR 10,000 per initiative; the Fund covers expenses incurred only after the results are announced (mid-December 2026) and will not reimburse expenses incurred beforehand. APPLY: create or log in to an account at https://candidate.kbs-frb.be and complete the online application form (it can be saved and completed in several stages). Applications are open 15 April 2026 to 30 September 2026; results announced mid-December 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Goethe-Institut: Africa-Europe Mobility Grants (15 September 2026 Cut-Off)
item.description:
Organisation: Goethe-Institut (with Expertise France and Institut francais)

Location: Mobility within Sub-Saharan Africa (primary focus) and between Sub-Saharan Africa and Europe

Award: Up to EUR 4,000 per mobility. Eligible costs: travel (including visa and insurance), accommodation, subsistence, digital costs, and top-up support for family, disability, and green mobility. No application fee.

Duration: Project-based short-term mobility; implementation between October 2025 and December 2027

Deadline: 15 Sep 2026

Sub-Saharan Component (Connect & Create) of the Africa-Europe Partnerships for Culture programme, an EUR 8 million 42-month initiative (2025-2028) implemented by the Goethe-Institut in partnership with Expertise France and Institut francais. PRIMARY FOCUS: transcontinental Africa-Africa short-term mobility for artists and culture professionals. European artists and organisations may also apply, provided they have an existing partnership with an African artist, institution or organisation. ACTIVITIES: artistic and professional development (research, co-creation/co-development, non-formal learning, building international professional relationships); cultural exchange and networking (residencies, exhibitions, conferences, workshops, training, collaborative international projects). Mobility may be physical, digital/virtual or hybrid. ELIGIBLE COUNTRIES: all Sub-Saharan Africa countries and all EU Member States. DISCIPLINES: visual arts, performing arts, music, literature, film, media arts, cultural heritage, design, architecture, fashion design. SCHEME TOTALS: up to 195 grants across the programme period; rolling intake with quarterly cut-offs. DEADLINE: this card is for the 15 September 2026 cut-off (other cut-offs each have their own card). APPLY via the Goethe-Institut Africa-Europe Partnerships page.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Grand Canyon Conservancy Residency 2027 (Artist / Astronomer / Environmental Educator)
item.description:
Organisation: Grand Canyon Conservancy (Grand Canyon National Park)

Location: Grand Canyon National Park, Arizona, United States

Award: Free on-site accommodation in the park, financial support (honorarium/stipend; amount varies by track and session - confirm on the track-specific page), plus marketing/exposure through the Conservancy and the National Park. Alumni network access. No application fee. Three tracks (Artist / Astronomer / Environmental Educator) - apply to one track per cycle.

Duration: Place-based residency at Grand Canyon National Park during 2027; dates and length vary by track; residents are expected to engage the public through programs, workshops, talks or similar

Deadline: 15 Jul 2026

Grand Canyon Conservancy's award-winning residency program invites artists, scientists, historians and educators to live on site at Grand Canyon National Park, pursue place-based research, and engage the public through meaningful programs that deepen understanding of the park's environmental, spiritual and cultural impact. Founded in 2020; competitive application + peer-panel review. THREE TRACKS: (1) Artist in Residence - for contemporary artists making interactive (immersive and/or participatory) work that shapes how people experience place; (2) Astronomer in Residence - for astronomers from any discipline who wish to share their knowledge and enthusiasm for dark skies; (3) Environmental Educator in Residence - for educators who use unique and inspiring methods to pique curiosity, prompt exploration, and build knowledge of the world. Residents receive free accommodation, financial support, and marketing/exposure; alumni return as part of a nationwide network. Read the individual track pages (linked from the main residency page) for track-specific eligibility, session lengths, stipend amounts, public-engagement expectations and any region/citizenship requirements before applying. APPLICATIONS OPEN: 15 May - 15 July 2026 for the 2027 cycle.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: EU-Japan Fest: Mobility Support 2026 (15 August 2026 Cut-Off)
item.description:
Organisation: EU-Japan Fest Japan Committee (in partnership with Culture Next)

Location: Travel between Japan and European Capitals of Culture / Culture Next member cities

Award: JPY 50,000 to JPY 150,000 per applicant, depending on applicant and travel category. No application fee.

Duration: Travel period April 2026 - September 2026 (applications must be submitted at least 6 weeks prior to travel)

Deadline: 15 Aug 2026

The EU-Japan Fest Japan Committee supports individuals and organisations developing projects between Japan and European Capitals of Culture (past, present or future) as well as other Culture Next member cities. PURPOSE: support travel for research, project planning, and collaboration, building creative networks aimed at realising Japan-Europe arts and culture projects. THEMES (for Japan-Europe collaborative projects): Youth Empowerment; Nature & Green; Diversity (DEI); Social Regeneration. Projects may include participants from other regions (e.g. Asia) joining a Japan-Europe project. TARGET APPLICANTS: members of European Capital of Culture teams; artists and project organisers (including curators, designers, etc.); researchers in culture/arts; social entrepreneurs. TIMING: applications must be submitted at least 6 weeks prior to the travel date; this cut-off covers travel in April 2026 - September 2026. DEADLINE: this card is for the 15 August 2026 cut-off (other cut-offs each have their own card). APPLY via the Culture Next collaboration page.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Faber Andorra Residency 2027
item.description:
Organisation: Faberllull

Location: La Massana, Andorra

Award: EUR 60 daily meal allowance plus fully equipped apartment (free) and 30 sqm visual/performing arts workshop. Travel and medical insurance NOT covered. No application fee.

Duration: 2-6 weeks, between February and November 2027

Deadline: 13 Jul 2026

Individual interdisciplinary residency in La Massana, Andorra, in a natural mountain setting. ELIGIBILITY: open worldwide to any professional from the arts, sciences and humanities with a project requiring focused working time; visual and audiovisual arts, crafts, photography, dramaturgy, performing arts, cinema, history, curating, criticism, philosophy, sociology, anthropology, journalism, science, research, biology, ecology, economics, law, writing and translation are all welcome. WHAT IS FUNDED: fully equipped apartment with work area, optional 30 sqm visual and performing arts workshop, and a daily allowance of EUR 60 to cover meals. Travel costs to/from Andorra and mandatory medical insurance are NOT covered. DURATION: 2 to 6 weeks; stays scheduled between February and November 2027. RESIDENCY ACTIVITIES: residents must propose at least two activities to carry out during the stay (one aimed at school and/or university students); at least one activity must be executed. SELECTION: priority to projects related to Andorra and to activities most attractive to the Andorran community; diversity of backgrounds and areas of work valued; previous Faberllull Olot/Faber Andorra residents may reapply but new candidates are preferred. APPLY: fill out the Faberllull form with a 1-page cover letter and a 2-page proposal of two possible activities; documentation accepted in Catalan, Spanish, English or French. TIMELINE: deadline 13 July 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Stimuleringsfonds: Open Call Fresh Perspectives 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Stimuleringsfonds Creatieve Industrie (Creative Industries Fund NL)

Location: Netherlands (Dutch fund; cross-sector collaboration)

Award: Two-phase grant. Phase 1: ten grants of EUR 10,000 (max 5 months, startup 1 December 2026 to 30 April 2027) to prepare, explore and prototype the project. Phase 2: ten grants of EUR 50,000 (max 12 months, implementation 1 September 2027 to 31 August 2028) for projects selected and fully completed in Phase 1. No application fee.

Duration: Phase 1 max 5 months; Phase 2 (if selected) max 12 months

Deadline: 25 Aug 2026

Open call from the Creative Industries Fund NL (Stimuleringsfonds) for cross-sector collaboration projects that offer new perspectives on current challenges such as climate, housing, polarisation, migration or inequality of opportunity. The procedure runs in two phases. In Phase 1, the applicant and partner use the period to prepare and kick off the project, further explore the theme, intended end product and collaboration, and investigate possibilities (including working on a prototype or proof of concept), concluding with a project plan, budget and collaboration agreement. Phase 2, open only to projects selected and fully completed in Phase 1, focuses on implementation and requires both a written application and a presentation to the advisory committee; projects may deliver various end products (an activity, intervention, event, publication, service, product or process) accompanied by a suitable form of knowledge sharing such as a presentation, symposium or publication. ELIGIBILITY: applicants working with at least one partner in cross-sector collaboration; see the fund's open-call page for full eligibility details. Deadline 25 August 2026, 16:00 CEST.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Camargo Foundation: Fellowship 2027-2028
item.description:
Organisation: Fondation Camargo

Location: Cassis, France (Mediterranean coast)

Award: EUR 3,500 stipend (EUR 350/week x 10 weeks); basic transportation costs; housing in the foundation's apartments for the residency duration. No application fee.

Duration: 10 weeks in 2027 or 2028

Deadline: 01 Oct 2026

Fondation Camargo's 10-week residency for writers, scholars and artists on the Mediterranean coast in Cassis, France. About 14 fellows per cycle. Open to writers across genres (poetry, fiction, nonfiction, translation), as well as scholars and artists pursuing serious research, writing or creative projects in the humanities, social sciences and arts. ELIGIBILITY: international writers, scholars and artists; no nationality restriction.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Stimuleringsfonds: Festivals Creative Industries 2027
item.description:
Organisation: Stimuleringsfonds Creatieve Industrie

Location: Netherlands

Award: Per-festival grant; specific maximum per Festivals scheme rules (cash subsidy, not in-kind)

Duration: Festival edition in 2027

Deadline: 23 Sep 2026

Annual scheme funding festival organisations in the creative industries (design, architecture, digital culture, fashion, e-culture, etc.) presenting a 2027 edition. Application window opens 25 August 2026 at 15:00 CEST and closes 23 September 2026 at 16:00 CEST. Supports both content programming and the organisational/curatorial running of a festival; applicants must be organisations rather than individuals, but the scheme is the natural home for any artist-led collective or platform that runs a critical-AI / digital-culture / surveillance-focused festival event. Note the wider 2026 context: Stimuleringsfonds is restructuring its grant offering for 2027, and several individual schemes (Design, Digital Culture) have been reduced to two rounds in 2026. The Festivals scheme is a separate annual track and one of the cleaner ways to access SCI funding for an event-format project.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).
